content: We can frame OpenAI's Computer-Using Agent (CUA) as a real-world memory-augmented Markov Decision Process, where every previous interaction with the graphical user interface (GUI) is stored in an external memory buffer to resolve the partial observability of dynamic on-screen states. This allows CUA to avoid the classic Markov assumption that all necessary state information is captured in the current screen, enabling more robust long-horizon tasks like filling multi-page online forms or debugging code across scattered application windows.
